purpose: Capture high-value concepts from predecessor AI projects that Project Theseus should preserve, port, or deliberately retire.

projects.role_in_lineage: SSD-first multimodal platform, background cognition, safety/advocate subsystems, device-first interaction.
Bare-metal neuro-symbolic runtime, thermal/resource awareness, hot-path gates, hive/work-stealing, first-party audio/runtime discipline.
Governed autonomy, self-modification contracts, apples-to-apples benchmarks, aerial/drone runtime bridge, manifest-first closure.
Compact local model family, architecture A/B discipline, first-party speech/router contracts, update lanes and wedges.
Contract-first trainer, registry/evidence graph, fixed-budget campaign DAGs, resource bounded orchestrator, telemetry.
Superloop, semantic compiler, trace-fabric training exchange, ATTD growth guard, hidden holdouts and eval track contracts.

port_candidates.concept: Use salience-scored Spark packets, priority queues, circadian/idle windows, and batch processing for background cognition.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make watchdog/autonomy corrections prioritize high-value conclusions, residual walls, teacher traces, and benchmark transitions over raw log noise.
acceptance_gates: context packets include salience fields
low-value packet compaction does not discard benchmark decisions
watchdog hard faults are ranked above routine status
dashboard exposes top active sparks

port_candidates.concept: Hot/warm/cold/archive memory tiers with background decay, merge, demotion, promotion, and compressed archive storage.
port_candidates.port_goal: Turn context packet compaction into an explicit tiered-memory policy, with cheap retrieval from important historical packets.
acceptance_gates: hot/warm/cold/archive counts reported
merge/decay decisions are deterministic
frontier/residual/teacher packets are protected by policy
compaction produces replayable summaries

port_candidates.concept: Request normalization, clarification, contract locks, quantitative risk gating, sycophancy probes, tribunal/adversary checks.
port_candidates.port_goal: Add a first-party input/governance preprocessor before teacher calls, self-edits, and high-risk autonomous goals.
acceptance_gates: ambiguous goals emit clarify/contract-lock packets
teacher prompts include normalized intent and risk envelope
high-risk goals route through safety/reflex arm
sycophancy/agreeability bias is tracked as a diagnostic

port_candidates.concept: Critical-module budgets, advisory gates, unsafe/fail-closed checks, hot-path microbench deltas, and HIL-lite envelopes.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make source and runtime hot-path regressions impossible to ignore during long autonomous runs.
acceptance_gates: critical module budget packets emitted
hot-loop benchmark deltas are reported per cycle
performance RED blocks long runs
CUDA/MLX/Hive worker changes require microbench evidence

port_candidates.concept: Decentralized work stealing, sharded state, idle worker slaving, and atomic per-worker negotiation.
port_candidates.port_goal: Move Theseus Hive from scheduled smoke chunks toward actual sharded training/eval chunks with resource-aware idle work stealing.
acceptance_gates: work packets declare shard ownership
idle workers request bounded chunks
chunk completion merges without global locks
worker telemetry tracks utilization and steal rate

port_candidates.concept: Manifest history, diffs, evidence tiers, holdout isolation, growth guard, autonomy freeze envelopes, replay/effect logs, shadow challengers.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make teacher self-edits and candidate promotions produce one closure-grade proof bundle every time.
acceptance_gates: self-edit branch emits effect log
promotion emits proof bundle with checkpoint/diff/hash links
rollback path is non-destructive and tested
shadow challenger keeps loser/winner lineage

port_candidates.concept: Separate practice and competition drone lanes, loopback bridges, blackbox command/ack/telemetry/video lineage, and sim2real campaign preflight.
port_candidates.port_goal: Turn drone RL from a learned local controller smoke into promotion-grade AI Grand Prix control evidence.
acceptance_gates: practice and competition profiles cannot be conflated
hardware commands remain approval-gated
blackbox parity packet records command/ack/telemetry/video hashes
sim2real residual clusters are escrowed

port_candidates.concept: Single-axis architecture candidate lifecycle with prime-cycle, recursive-core, hemispheric readout, control-placement, and shadow variants.
port_candidates.port_goal: Give the teacher and local search a small, pre-declared motif library so architectural changes remain minimal and comparable.
acceptance_gates: motifs are represented as named low-blast-radius experiment families
single-axis before coupled changes is enforced
near-miss reruns require declared budget
retired motifs are not reopened without explicit exception

port_candidates.concept: Native streaming speech engine with audio chunks, transcript hypotheses, shared latent tokens, speech-to-cognition routing, and parity reports.
port_candidates.port_goal: Keep STT/TTS as Theseus-owned head/router capability, trained from licensed data, with no external speech inference.
acceptance_gates: audio packet contract is versioned
STT/TTS bootstrap artifacts are trainable local state
speech route evidence joins normal routing memory
roundtrip eval tracks WER/CER/latency and synthesis intelligibility

port_candidates.concept: Manifest-defined campaign DAGs, asset/rule separation, deterministic streaming data plane, topology-aware resume, and fixed-budget reports.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make every Theseus long run a replayable campaign with explicit assets, rules, datasets, envs, stages, budgets, and resume topology.
acceptance_gates: cycle plan is exported before execution
benchmark asset and scoring rule are separate records
dataset/env mixture hash is persisted
resume topology compatibility is checked

port_candidates.concept: Candidate evidence graph plus deterministic keep/discard/hold/retry research ledger.
port_candidates.port_goal: Stop treating experiments as loose reports; every candidate should have a durable verdict row and proof artifact references.
acceptance_gates: every architecture/run candidate has candidate_digest and baseline_digest
keep/discard decisions require proof refs
ledger rows are append-only and sorted
dashboard summarizes current champion and challengers

port_candidates.concept: Trace capsules with reward components, episode boundaries, replay contracts, data minimization, and quality payout policy.
port_candidates.port_goal: Convert real Theseus work traces into trainable, contamination-checked learning packets for future self-repair and tool use.
acceptance_gates: trace packets include reward components and episode fields
holdout fingerprints reject contaminated traces
golden replay bundles are created for regressions
compute-market work credit uses quality-weighted packets

port_candidates.concept: Semantic intent IR, localized repair apply contracts, plan graphs, compiler passes, and effect graph bundles.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make user goals and teacher patches compile through a small semantic intent/effect graph before source or runtime mutation.
acceptance_gates: goal packets include intent graph and side-effect envelope
repair patches include localized effect graph
loop-closure tools are generated from intent patterns
verification compares intended vs observed effects

port_candidates.concept: Predeclared eval-track contracts for RLM/verifier-centric reasoning, MTP, KV policy, differential attention, BitNet, context freeze/thaw, and other architecture lanes.
port_candidates.port_goal: Expand Theseus architecture search only as named contract-backed tracks with cheap feasibility probes first.
acceptance_gates: each track has a smoke feasibility probe
model growth gate maps residual wall to a named track
teacher cannot introduce unnamed architecture families
retirement criteria are explicit

port_candidates.concept: PlanForge decomposes goals into contracts, risk envelopes, DAGs, and critical-path schedules instead of a single linear loop.
port_candidates.port_goal: Let long autonomous runs schedule benchmark pressure, teacher review, synthetic data, RL training, and maintenance as a dependency-aware critical path.
acceptance_gates: cycle report includes goal DAG nodes and blockers
scheduler distinguishes critical path from background maintenance
Hive workers can consume independent ready nodes
teacher work is never scheduled while cheaper unblocked local work exists

port_candidates.concept: Synaptic capability strengths decay or reinforce based on verified use, turning permissions into earned, revisable trust.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make arms, tools, and Hive workers gain or lose capability envelopes from outcomes instead of static registration alone.
acceptance_gates: arm/tool trust scores decay over disuse or failures
successful verified use reinforces bounded permissions
high-risk permissions require recent proof
dashboard shows decayed, probationary, and trusted capabilities

port_candidates.concept: Fractal temporal versioning, timeline replay, temporal assertions, and ghost overlays for debugging long-horizon behavior.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make checkpoints, drone/game rollouts, and self-edits replayable with temporal assertions that catch regressions before promotion.
acceptance_gates: checkpoint deltas carry replay assertions
RL traces expose timeline checkpoints
promotion closure replays critical assertions
failed assertions enter residual escrow with timeline IDs

port_candidates.concept: WhiteCell keeps local threat memory for prompt shielding, code auditing, traffic guarding, and policy hardening.
port_candidates.port_goal: Add a local security memory that can block repeated unsafe teacher prompts, bad network sources, and risky patches.
acceptance_gates: security incidents persist as reusable local patterns
teacher prompts are screened against threat memory
network source risk updates license/source gates
risky source edits require explicit safety evidence

port_candidates.concept: Runtime coherence and delirium metrics detect internal instability before ordinary benchmarks show obvious failure.
port_candidates.port_goal: Add a cheap health signal for stuck, noisy, thrashing, or self-contradictory autonomy behavior that can pause, rotate, or call the teacher.
acceptance_gates: watchdog reports coherence and delirium scores
repeated low coherence triggers bounded correction
model growth gate treats instability as evidence
dashboard displays instability trend

port_candidates.concept: Active-inference loops and compact world models choose actions that reduce surprise under resource constraints.
port_candidates.port_goal: Give drone, game, and tool-use arms a shared local world-model/action-selection primitive instead of isolated scripted baselines.
acceptance_gates: world-model learner emits prediction error and action value
drone/game runners can train/evaluate the learner
transfer planner can reuse learned priors
resource limits are part of action selection

port_candidates.concept: Zero-copy context handles, predictive prefetch, and context-horizon diff-apply keep long-running cognition fast.
port_candidates.port_goal: Reduce context/report churn by passing stable packet references and prefetching only the likely next pressure surface.
acceptance_gates: large reports are referenced by digest where possible
next-frontier artifacts are prefetched before rotation
context compaction avoids duplicate report bodies
dashboard API exposes packet refs and summaries

port_candidates.concept: Hardware-in-the-loop and emulator-lite gates validate host/device interaction before real-world control is trusted.
port_candidates.port_goal: Require emulator/sim evidence for drones, robots, games, phones, and weak Hive clients before live control or promotion.
acceptance_gates: device/world adapters declare sim, emulator, or live mode
live mode is blocked without sim smoke evidence
HIL failures produce escrow items
promotion reports include HIL parity status

port_candidates.concept: Proxy traces and benchmark truth audits fail closed on contaminated rows, fabricated results, missing raw outputs, and runtime identity mismatch.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make every benchmark/adapter score prove it came from the intended checkpoint, tokenizer, prompt template, runtime path, and raw outputs.
acceptance_gates: score reports include checkpoint/runtime/template hashes
missing raw outputs block candidate promotion
contaminated or fabricated rows are classified fail-closed
truth audit summary is visible in candidate gate

port_candidates.concept: Formal contract mapping binds runtime invariants, deterministic coupling hashes, proof obligations, and rollback completeness.
port_candidates.port_goal: Give self-edit and promotion closure a machine-checkable invariant registry before architecture changes become routine.
acceptance_gates: self-edit plans list affected invariants
runtime coupling hashes are emitted
rollback completeness is checked before merge
unmapped invariants block high-risk edits

port_candidates.concept: Veritas claim validation, novelty scoring, falsification probes, and discovery evaluation for new data, benchmarks, and synthetic cases.
port_candidates.port_goal: Prevent resource pantry growth from becoming a pile of duplicate, weak, or unverified sources.
acceptance_gates: new sources get novelty and claim-validation rows
synthetic data proposals include falsification probes
duplicate benchmark/data concepts are rejected
pantry entries carry discovery confidence

port_candidates.concept: Anti-expert negative routing and tribunal sessions preserve dissent and abstain when a specialist is known to fail a residual class.
port_candidates.port_goal: Teach the router not only which arm to pick, but which arm to avoid for known failure clusters.
acceptance_gates: routing memory stores negative expertise
residual clusters can veto arms
tribunal disagreement is logged
router eval includes anti-route accuracy

port_candidates.concept: TaskSpell locks immutable task contracts with acceptance tests, non-goals, budgets, risks, and route/runtime/evidence records.
port_candidates.port_goal: Compile user and teacher goals into stable task contracts before autonomous execution or self-editing begins.
acceptance_gates: goals emit immutable task lock hashes
teacher prompts carry acceptance tests and non-goals
contract drift is reported
goal completion requires evidence rows for all locked tests

port_candidates.concept: Macro/loop closure promotes only after replay verification and counterexample search, then keeps usage/failure telemetry.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make benchmark ingestion, residual analysis, smoke setup, and checkpoint repair close into tools without creating brittle scripts.
acceptance_gates: tool candidates include counterexample tests
promotion requires replay on old and synthetic cases
tool telemetry can retire stale macros
failed macro cases enter residual escrow

port_candidates.concept: Low-rank lane adapter banks for math, code, speech, tool use, and other capabilities are promoted only when interference stays bounded.
port_candidates.port_goal: Turn arm transfer learning into concrete reusable adapters with interference matrices and lane-safe promotion gates.
acceptance_gates: adapter artifacts declare source/target lanes
interference matrix is reported
router loads adapters by lane and task
promotion requires no critical lane regression

port_candidates.concept: Probe-before-specialist router uses reflex, grounded, deep, and hard-stop bands plus a bounded deep-route burst budget.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make text, voice, and agentic endpoint requests follow the same route path and spend deep cognition only under evidence.
acceptance_gates: router reports band and burst-budget use
spoken turns cannot bypass routing/governance
hard-stop band blocks unsafe tool paths
deep-route budget resets only by policy

port_candidates.concept: Runtime capability resolution separates planner intent from CUDA, MLX, CPU, and fallback backend selection.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make every training/eval request resolve through an explicit backend contract so Mac MLX, Windows CUDA, CPU, and Hive fallback stay comparable.
acceptance_gates: pressure runners declare required capabilities
backend resolver emits selected runtime and fallback reason
CUDA/MLX/CPU scores include backend metadata
bad fallback does not masquerade as target runtime

port_candidates.concept: Dataset recipe discovery/scaffolding turns candidate datasets into validated manifests, splits, checksums, and smoke samplers.
port_candidates.port_goal: Let Theseus add open training data with less teacher involvement and fewer one-off loader fixes.
acceptance_gates: new data source creates recipe card automatically
recipe smoke test verifies access and checksum
license and contamination gates are attached
sampler can draw tiny governed batches

port_candidates.concept: RL trace export into interchange formats such as RLDS/Minari keeps game, drone, and tool-use episodes reusable.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make all RL/environment traces portable training assets rather than runner-specific logs.
acceptance_gates: RL episodes export observation/action/reward/done rows
export manifests include license/source metadata
transfer planner can consume exported traces
episode replay smoke passes

port_candidates.concept: Training advisors and live operator panels diagnose stalls, runtime choices, and next actions without waiting for teacher reasoning.
port_candidates.port_goal: Show actionable local advice in the dashboard when benchmarks stall, resources bottleneck, or teacher calls are waiting.
acceptance_gates: dashboard exposes top recommended local correction
advisor output is deterministic from reports
teacher-needed reasons are separated from local fixes
operator can see current blocker and next frontier

port_candidates.concept: World adapter and world-execution job contracts provide deterministic pause/resume/cancel/status, adapter coverage, and checkpointed branch lineage.
port_candidates.port_goal: Standardize drone, game, web, robot, and simulation environments behind one replayable world job interface.
acceptance_gates: world runners implement pause/resume/cancel/status
adapter coverage matrix is emitted
branch checkpoints have stable hashes
missing required world capabilities fail closed

port_candidates.concept: Compute mode acceptance proves local/cloud/auto routing parity, fallback behavior, resolver latency, and cost boundaries.
port_candidates.port_goal: Keep Theseus Hive resource-aware as it grows across CUDA, MLX, CPU, public, private, and rented compute.
acceptance_gates: compute resolver scenarios are smoke-tested
degraded local fallback is deterministic
cost budget can force local execution
resolver p95 latency is reported

port_candidates.concept: Bridge-adapter lanes must promote to native runtime when latency, quality, or policy-control risk becomes high.
port_candidates.port_goal: Prevent important benchmark, RL, trace, and data lanes from staying forever as brittle Python/shell adapter glue.
acceptance_gates: adapter cards track native-promotion priority
high-value lanes require native-roadmap entries
native promotion smoke excludes legacy command paths
performance gate flags adapter overhead

port_candidates.concept: g5xr captures browser/emulator gameplay events, trace batches, AI copilot turns, and BYO-ROM metadata without binary ROM ingestion.
port_candidates.port_goal: Turn local owned game collections and emulator sessions into governed RL/eval traces and regression suites.
acceptance_gates: game runners store metadata without copying ROM binaries into git
frame/event traces export to replayable episodes
BYO license/source policy is explicit
AI copilot/gameplay turns are contamination-checked before training

port_candidates.concept: Benchmark bounty flow verifies candidate benchmarks for duplicate IDs, duplicate commands, canonical fingerprints, tag novelty, and required field quality.
port_candidates.port_goal: Give Theseus a community/public-hive path for adding benchmark pressure safely without flooding the pantry with junk.
acceptance_gates: benchmark submissions get novelty and quality scores
duplicate command/source/fingerprint checks run
accepted submissions create adapter cards
rejected submissions include deterministic reasons

port_candidates.concept: ORCP compression lanes use deterministic round-trip, identity, compression-ratio, and Hutter-style gates as pressure surfaces.
port_candidates.port_goal: Add compression as a native intelligence pressure lane for memory, context, checkpoint, and data representation quality.
acceptance_gates: compression runner has round-trip smoke
ratio and decoder-size metrics are logged
archive identity integrates with checkpoint deltas
failed deterministic replay blocks promotion

port_candidates.concept: USB/serial endpoint uses JSONL request/response frames, installer autodetection, and a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint for weak devices.
port_candidates.port_goal: Make phones, microcontrollers, robots, and lightweight clients able to talk to the Hive without bespoke integrations.
acceptance_gates: JSONL device protocol validates frame version/kind/id
weak clients can proxy to local Hive endpoint
installer mode does not require terminal
device requests use the same route/governance path

port_candidates.concept: Pre-training readiness checks evidence persistence, reward formalization, action/observation contracts, episode boundaries, contamination, deterministic replay, streaming reliability, and semantic watchdogs.
port_candidates.port_goal: Before long runs, prove each active training lane has contracts, rewards, episode boundaries, replay, and contamination gates.
acceptance_gates: readiness report is per-lane, not only global
missing reward or done signal blocks RL long runs
streaming checksums are verified before training
semantic watchdog warnings gate candidate promotion
